summary: Hell‑In‑A‑Bucket fireworks, tight High Time, crisp soundboard
blurb: The show bursts with a raging Hell In A Bucket and a punchy High Time. Jerry delivers incendiary guitar fills while Brent’s keyboards add depth, and the second set rides through Fire On The Mountain into an expansive Other One. Overall performance is energetic and consistently strong.
review: September 24, 1987 at Philadelphia’s Spectrum captures the Dead in high‑gear mode. Opening with a bright Touch of Grey, the first set quickly escalates – High Time rockets forward, My Brother Esau slides into Bird Song, and Bob Weir roars through Big River and Hell In A Bucket with unbridled energy. The second set is a showcase: Box of Rain opens the flow, then an incendiary Hell In A Bucket segues into a soaring Fire On The Mountain, followed by a spacey stretch that erupts into a marathon The Other One. Throughout, Jerry’s guitar fireworks and Brent’s rich keyboard textures keep the momentum blazing, while Phil’s steady bass underpins the whole night. With crisp C‑Miller remaster soundboard fidelity, this performance stands out as one of the late‑’80s highlights.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the show as excellent or outstanding, highlighting Hell In A Bucket, High Time, Fire On The Mountain and strong guitar/keyboard work. No consistent negative comments appear; mixed sentiments are neutral. Positive language such as “excellent”, “epic” and “outstanding” across three of four analyses justifies a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: C‑Miller remastered soundboard provides crystal‑clear, high‑verve audio with no dropouts, capturing all highlighted moments in the best possible fidelity.

key_highlights: Raging Hell In A Bucket that drives the set forward
Punchy High Time and tight first‑set flow
Fire On The Mountain segue into a spacey, expansive The Other One
Brent’s deep keyboard coloring and Jerry’s incendiary guitar fills

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers incendiary fills and guitar fireworks throughout, keeping the energy blazing; a few notes mention his voice sounding strained but his playing remains solid.
band_performance.Phil: Provides steady, supportive bass that underpins the high‑energy jams, praised for being reliable and plucky.
band_performance.Bob: Takes lead on Big River and screams through Hell In A Bucket, with vocal effects adding character to the performance.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboard tones receive praise for depth and coloring, enhancing songs like High Time and The Other One.
